codeReview.summary: The worker applied content-visibility: auto with per-container contain-intrinsic-size estimates to 14 scrollable containers across the app. The selection is well-targeted toward impactful long-scroll areas, and the risky drag-and-drop/PDF viewer containers using scrollableContainerRef were correctly left untouched. The chosen intrinsic sizes (400-800px) are reasonable for the affected UI surfaces. Build and lint were reported clean in the handoff. A few unrelated formatting-style edits and one harmless sync-to-async onClick change increased diff noise, but they do not block the feature and VAL-RENDERING-001 is satisfied.

issues.description: content-visibility: auto was added to the main content wrapper that fills the viewport. Because that container itself is typically on-screen, the optimization likely yields little benefit compared with the inner long-scroll containers it hosts.

The onClick handler changed from a synchronous arrow function to an async arrow function. React ignores the return value, so this is functionally harmless, but it is outside the stated scope of the feature.
This file includes substantial unrelated Tailwind class reordering alongside the content-visibility change. The extra churn is not harmful but makes the review diff noisier than necessary.
The diff includes unrelated Tailwind class reordering in addition to the new content-visibility classes, indicating formatter noise beyond the feature's core change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission and repo guidance do not say whether workers should avoid formatter-driven class reordering when making focused performance changes. This feature's diff picked up unrelated Tailwind class ordering edits in several touched files, creating avoidable review noise.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/remix/app/components/general/webhook-logs-sheet.tsx shows widespread class reordering beyond the new content-visibility classes; similar unrelated ordering changes appear in organisation-member-invite-dialog.tsx, public-profile-template-manage-dialog.tsx, and team-group-create-dialog.tsx.

summary: PASS. Appropriate long scrollable containers were updated with content-visibility: auto and sensible intrinsic sizes, while risky measurement/drag-related viewer containers were avoided. The only findings are minor diff-scope issues and one low-value application on the envelope editor main wrapper.
